issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query calculates lifetime value without considering the transaction status.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Missing filter for total orders
issues.problem: The query counts all transactions without considering the status.
issues.fix: COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id ELSE NULL END) as total_orders

issues.title: Missing comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and calculations.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.problem: The naming of aliases and columns is inconsistent.
issues.fix: Use consistent naming conventions for aliases and columns.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id ELSE NULL END) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had issues with revenue calculation and total orders count. It also lacked comments and had inconsistent naming.

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is calculated incorrectly because failed_count is not defined in the SELECT clause but used in the calculation.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: LEFT JOIN (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as failed_count FROM fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED' GROUP BY transaction_date) f2 ON f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which makes it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT t.transaction_date, COALESCE(f2.failed_count, 0) as failed_count, COUNT(*) as total_count, COALESCE(f2.failed_count, 0) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) as failure_rate FROM fact_transactions t LEFT JOIN (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as failed_count FROM fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED' GROUP BY transaction_date) f2 ON f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date GROUP BY t.transaction_date ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query has a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation and a performance issue with the correlated subquery. It also lacks comments for readability.

issues.title: Missing filter for transaction status
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status, which means it will include all transactions regardless of their status.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, which could be helpful for future maintainers.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose and logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, m.merchant_name, SUM(t.amount) AS merchant_spend, COUNT(*) AS txn_count FROM dim_customer c JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE c.tier = 'GOLD' AND t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email, m.merchant_name ORDER BY merchant_spend DESC;
review.summary: The query is mostly correct but misses a filter for transaction status and lacks comments for readability.

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query sums all transaction amounts without filtering for completed transactions only.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue

issues.title: Potential performance issue
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to less readable and potentially less performant queries.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose and logic.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t
JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and a readability issue with lack of comments. It also used implicit join syntax which could be improved for performance and readability.
